description: The provided image is unreadable (placeholder only). Based on the paper context and text extraction summary, this figure likely displays scanning electron microscopy (SEM) micrographs comparing the microstructure of Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C low-density steel alloyed with 0 wt%, 1 wt%, and 3 wt% copper. The subfigures would respectively correspond to the 0Cu (base alloy), 1Cu, and 3Cu samples, all processed via vacuum induction melting, homogenization at 1200°C for 2 hours, and multipass hot rolling above 850°C followed by air cooling. According to the text extraction reports, the 0Cu sample exhibits NbC carbides (MC type) with an average size of 0.52 μm and a count of 1740 distributed on austenite grain surfaces; the 1Cu sample shows refined carbides (0.51 μm) with increased density (2377 particles) and slight Cu enrichment at grain boundaries; while the 3Cu sample demonstrates significant accumulation of elemental Cu particles at grain boundaries with a sharp reduction in carbide number to 876. These microstructural variations support the core scientific conclusion that Cu addition alters carbide precipitation behavior by hindering carbon diffusion, and the resulting Cu-enriched grain boundaries create microgalvanic corrosion couples with carbides, thereby deteriorating the corrosion resistance in 3.5 wt% NaCl solution as evidenced by increasing corrosion rates from 0.0546 mm/a (0Cu) to 0.092 mm/a (3Cu).
